deck_quality.explanation: Deck is acceptable but has room to improve — consider removing more basic cards and prioritising upgrades at rest sites.
evidence: 10/20 cards are basic starters (50%) — these dilute every draw
Only 5/20 cards upgraded (25%) by floor 33 — missed upgrade opportunities

defense.explanation: Defense is solid with 7 block cards — enough to handle burst turns reliably.
evidence: Block card count is adequate: 7/20 (35%)
Only 0/7 block cards upgraded (0%) — upgraded block is much more efficient

scaling.explanation: Scaling is well-established with 2 unique The Silent scaling cards — the deck can handle long fights.
evidence: Only 2 scaling card(s) in Act 3 — end-game fights require strong scaling

boss_readiness.explanation: Well-prepared for The Collector. HP was healthy going into the fight, and the deck had tools to handle the encounter.
evidence: Entered The Collector with only 37/72 HP (51%) — wanted ≥ 60%

Boss insight: The Collector summons torch-head minions that apply Mega Debuff. If you can't clear them quickly the debuff stacking leads to unblockable burst damage. AoE or fast single-target damage helps.

pathing.explanation: Pathing was sensible — good elite/rest balance across acts.
evidence: Elite pathing was reasonable across acts
6 rest sites left HP below 75% — pathing may be too greedy

relic_synergy.explanation: Relic suite is strong — 9 relics with good energy and synergy support.
evidence: Relic count is on par: 9 relics by floor 33
No energy relic or energy-generating relic by Act 3 — 4 energy/turn may be insufficient
Scaling cards are backed by scaling relics — good synergy
